algorithm:
This algorithm selects the next customer based on a dynamic weighting scheme that integrates adaptive learning 
    from historical truck wait times, proximity to time window thresholds, and balanced network dispersion to optimize route efficiency and minimize delays.

This algorithm employs dynamic prioritization by calculating a prospective score based on inverse distance_weighted prioritization, 
    urgency in time window constraints, and congestion avoidance, focusing on reducing overlap in clusters while ensuring timely visits.

The new algorithm uses a dynamic scoring system that updates scores based on the number of nearby unvisited customers, proximity of time windows closing, and the cumulative penalties if multiple customers are missed, encouraging clustering and strategic waiting.
The new algorithm incorporates an urgency factor directly proportional to the inverse square of the time window proximity in its score function to emphasize time-critical visits more heavily.
An algorithm that prioritizes customers based on time window urgency and proximity while considering potential traffic delays and optimizing based on a dynamic scoring function.
